name: Anti-Hallucination
description: Mandatory visual inventory step: list all edges/nodes with CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN labels before reasoning
content:
Before solving any problem involving a diagram or graph, follow this MANDATORY procedure:

Step 1 — Visual Inventory: List every node and edge visible in the image. For each edge, write "(u, v, w=X) [CONFIDENT]" or "(u, v, w=?) [UNCERTAIN — label unclear]". NEVER skip an edge because its label is ambiguous; mark it UNCERTAIN instead.

Step 2 — Text Priority: If the question text contains an adjacency list, edge weights, or structural data, treat it as ground truth and override UNCERTAIN visual readings.

Step 3 — Reconciliation: State the final confirmed graph structure you will use.

Step 4 — Solution: Solve using only the reconciled structure. Never invent edges not seen in Step 1 or Step 2.
